thinking: I face a mostly empty room with wood flooring and white walls, where a single female customer stands against the right wall; I move steadily closer while she shifts her legs and keeps her body angled toward me. As I continue forward, the left side of the room comes into view with a yellow rectangular table, matching chairs, blue lower cabinets, a sink, countertop appliances, and a tall light-colored refrigerator. The customer walks from the wall toward the near side of the table, places her hands on the tabletop, bends forward, then lowers herself into the chair on the far/right side of the table. I stop near the table edge facing her, with my hand briefly visible in front, while she settles into a seated waiting posture.
caption: CLERK: My apparent objective was to guide or monitor the customer to a seating area, and I achieved it by walking toward her and accompanying her to the table. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want a place to sit or wait, expressing this by approaching the table, bracing on it with her hands, and then sitting down in the chair.
